reasoning: The Cleveland Fed's 30-year expected inflation series (EXPINF30YR) updates monthly. For the first resolution date (Jan 6, 2026), there is a significant chance that the January data release will not yet be published (releases typically follow inflation data mid-month or occur after the first week). If it has not updated by Jan 6, the value will be identical to the Dec 7 value, which means it will NOT have increased. Thus, the probability for Jan 6 is substantially lower than 0.5, reflecting this 'no update' risk. For the distant date (March 7), the series will have updated several times. Since the series behaves similarly to a random walk but has shown slight upward momentum over the past 4 months, the probability of it being higher in March compared to December is around 0.51. This respects the instruction to have uncertainty increase with the horizon, pulling the distant estimate closer to 0.50.
